nodes.context: North American neo-Nazi organization adhering to Christian Identity, originally based in Idaho.
Christian Identity church founded by Wesley A. Swift, later controlled by Richard Butler.
White supremacist terrorist group formed by Robert Jay Mathews, committed domestic terrorism in 1983-1984.
Group formed by Dennis McGiffen, inspired by The Order, arrested before carrying out plans.
Splinter group of Aryan Nations led by Jay Faber, based in New York and later Texas.
Splinter group of Aryan Nations led by Paul Mullet, had branches in 17 states.

nodes.standard_name: Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood
nodes.original_text_name: Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood
nodes.context: Enforcement/terrorist wing of Aryan Nations according to government.
Black religious organization; Butler claimed Louis Farrakhan as an ally.
Islamist terrorist group; August Kreis III attempted to form an alliance with Aryan Nations.

edges.context: Aryan Nations was founded as the paramilitary wing of the Church of Jesus Christ–Christian.
The Order was formed by Robert Jay Mathews, who visited Aryan Nations compound, and included Aryan Nations members.
The New Order was inspired by The Order and formed by Dennis McGiffen, who had ties to Aryan Nations.
Aryan Nations Revival was a splinter group of Aryan Nations led by Jay Faber.
Crusaders of Yahweh was a splinter group of Aryan Nations led by Paul Mullet.

edges.source: Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood
edges.context: Government concluded it was the enforcement/terrorist wing of Aryan Nations.
Butler claimed Louis Farrakhan of Nation of Islam as a black ally in his plan for racial mini-states.
August Kreis III attempted to form an Aryan Nations–al Qaeda alliance in 2005.
